prevalence.value: 10-20% annual incidence in children (episodes; mostly viral)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical assessment; Centor/McIsaac criteria support GAS suspicion; rapid antigen test/culture when available; avoid antibiotics for likely viral pharyngitis

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High symptom resolution; antibiotic stewardship is key

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS uses clinical criteria; rapid testing/culture availability varies
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care for viral cases; penicillin/amoxicillin for suspected/confirmed GAS; treat rheumatic fever prevention in appropriate cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Overuse of antibiotics in some settings and limited rapid testing availability
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Wide APS coverage and availability of penicillin/amoxicillin
